hub_subtitle: Inglês para o trabalho — do aperto de mão ao acordo fechado, em 7 aulas

lesson.menu_desc: Miss a word in a meeting and stay in the conversation -- ask to repeat, slow down, and explain -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 5 -- When You Don't Understand
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ziláudio | Lesson 5 -- When You Don't Understand
lesson.grammar_point: clarification: asking someone to repeat, slow down, and explain meaning

listenings.text: Good morning everyone, and thank you for joining today's quarterly meeting. Before we begin, a quick note on the agenda. We have three main points to cover: first, the harvest results from last season; second, the new logistics contract for grain transport; and third, the export price forecast for the next quarter. I know some of these topics are technical, so please stop me at any time if something is not clear. We will move fast, but there is no problem if you need me to repeat a point or explain a word. The meeting starts in five minutes, in room two. Please have your agenda ready.

Hi Zilaudio, this is Karen. I am calling about our meeting on Thursday. I want to make sure everything is clear for you before we start. If I speak too fast, please tell me, and I will slow down. And if you hear a word you do not know, just ask me what it means. That is completely normal, and it is a smart thing to do. Please read the agenda before the meeting, so you know the main points. See you on Thursday, and thank you.
